title: AI Wildlife Platform Transforms Global Conservation
content:
EarthRanger is a wildlife-management platform that has expanded to a remarkable degree and is saving animals' lives. Launched in 2015, EarthRanger is now an AI-integrated data visualization and analysis software. It provides a real-time view of tagged animals, rangers, and other assets like vehicles and fences. The system also tracks poachers, generating alerts that can mobilize field teams so they can intervene quickly if they see evidence of trouble.

Developed by Save the Elephants with Vulcan, the investment company of a prominent philanthropist, the platform tracks everything from pumas in Chile to chimpanzees in Rwandan rainforests. Mobile-ready and device-agnostic, it enables easy data sharing to bolster collaboration. EarthRanger is also free, but its potential economic impact is vast. It has become a tool arguably indispensable to the global ecotourism market, particularly in Africa where recent conservation efforts have helped halt steep declines in the numbers of elephants and rhinoceroses.

Recently, major environmental titans announced plans to join forces and form the SMART-EarthRanger Conservation Alliance (SERCA). This alliance unites the world’s two most widely used conservation management software platforms into a single system. As SERCA gradually rolls out, it will add a burgeoning number of tools to help researchers. The ultimate goal is to package these workflows as tools that can be used not only by academics and computer programmers but also by policymakers to ensure the safety of global wildlife.
